title: Vegetable tagine
subtitle: Moroccan spiced vegetable stew with chickpeas and preserved lemon
excerpt: Sweet potato, carrot, courgette, and chickpeas braised together with ras el hanout, saffron, and preserved lemon into a fragrant, warming stew. The preserved lemon adds a characteristic Moroccan note that lifts the whole dish. Vegan, and complete as a main course over couscous.
sourceNotes: Vegetable tagines are a substantial part of Moroccan cooking, not a concession to vegetarianism. The combination of root vegetables, legumes, and dried fruit or preserved citrus in a spiced broth is a cooking tradition in its own right. This recipe draws on the seven-vegetable couscous tradition of the Moroccan Friday meal.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.

content.text: The cooking order matters here: harder root vegetables go in first, softer courgette last. Cut everything to roughly the same size (3 cm) so the vegetables finish cooking together. The sweet potato adds natural sweetness and body; the chickpeas add protein and soak up the spiced broth.

The preserved lemon is added at the end, not at the beginning — its delicate citrus-salt flavour should remain distinct in the finished dish. Using it early turns it bitter and loses the characteristic note that separates this from a generic vegetable stew.

items.prepNote: medium, peeled and cut into 3 cm cubes

content.text: Heat the {{olive-oil}} in a large, heavy casserole over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and cook for 8 minutes until soft. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ras-el-hanout}}, and {{cinnamon-ground}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the {{sweet-potato}}, {{carrot}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, saffron water, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and 300 ml of cold water. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 15 minutes. Add the {{courgette}} and {{chickpeas}}. Cover and cook for a further 15 minutes until all vegetables are tender. Remove from the heat. Stir in the {{preserved-lemon}} rind and {{coriander}}. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve over couscous.

items.cause: Courgette added too early or heat too high
items.fix: Add courgette only in the last 15 minutes — it cooks much faster than root vegetables. Maintain a gentle simmer, not a boil

items.cause: Too much water or lid kept off
items.fix: Remove the lid for the last 10 minutes to reduce. The sweet potato should break down slightly and thicken the sauce naturally

items.cause: Insufficient salt or ras el hanout too mild
items.fix: Season assertively — the chickpeas and sweet potato absorb salt. Taste after adding the preserved lemon and adjust

content.text: Vegetable tagines in Morocco are not a substitute for meat — they are a category in their own right, served as a main course on meatless days or alongside a meat dish as one of several courses. The seven-vegetable couscous, served on Fridays and at celebrations, is the most elaborate form: seven different vegetables braised together in a saffron broth. This simpler four-vegetable version captures the same flavour principle. Serve with couscous and harissa on the side.
